solutions.solution: (b) If there is one triangle at the bottom, there would be three triangles on the top.
(d) From the figures (i) and (ii), it is clear that the two surfaces are common to both figures i.e., 4 and 5. So, the surfaces left contains 3 and 6 dots, which are opposite to each other.
(d) From positions II and III, Common dots = 2 and 5 So, face opposite to the face having 1 dot will be the face having 6 dots.
(d) From positions I and III. Common points = 2 [at the common (right) face] So, face opposite to the face having 1 dot will be the face having 6 dots.
(c) From positions I, II and IV. Adjacent faces of the face having 3 dots = 1 2, 4 and 6 So, face opposite to the face having 3 dots will be the face having 5 dots.

(d) Looking at the first two figures we see that 2 is opposite to 6 and 3 is opposite to 4. The third figure tells us that 5 is opposite to 1. Thus, the numbers A and B, which are adjacent to 5 could be any of the following four: 6 and 4, 4 and 2, 2 and 3, and 3 and 6. out of these only 2 and 3 is given as a option, so (a) is the right answer.
(c) Common letter = J [at the same (right) face] Clearly, S is opposite to V.
(c) From positions II and III, Common letters = P and Y Clearly, A is opposite to L.
(c) In a cube, any pair of adjoining sides will have two common adjoining sides which will be opposite to each other. In the given question, these two sides are A and H. Hence, it is opposite to A.
(c) From, positions I and II, Common letters = M and S Clearly, W is opposite to D.

(c) From both the positions I and II, Common colour = White [at different faces] From position I (clokwise): White, Blue, Green; From position II: White, Yellow, Black. Clearly, green is opposite to black. It means green is at the bottom, and black is at the top.
(d) From the given four positions of a single dice. Faces adjacent to face having green colour = Orange, red, white, silver Clearly, violet is opposite to green.
